content.text:
Decision Support Systems
Semester:
3  |
Type:
UEF32  |
VHS:
63h  |
Weekly hours:
4h30  |
Lecture:
1h30  |
Tutorial:
1h30  |
Lab:
1h30
Personal work VHS:
42h  |
Coefficient:
3  |
Credits:
4
Teaching Objectives
Master the architectures and techniques of decision support systems.
Course Content (21h)
Chapter I – Introduction
Course objectives
Definition of decision support systems
Decision typology: strategic, tactical, operational
General architecture of a DSS (data sources, models, interface)
Chapter II – Data and Preprocessing
Nature and types of data used in decision-making
Data preprocessing: cleaning, normalisation, coding
Introduction to dissimilarity indices (Euclidean, Manhattan, etc.)
Chapter III – Decision Criteria and Discriminant Methods
Decision criteria: utility, gain, risk minimisation
Fisher's criterion: optimal class separation
Linear discriminant functions: formulation and interpretation
Chapter IV – Statistical Methods for Decision-Making
Supervised statistical methods:
Bayesian decision
Probabilistic methods and classification rules
Unsupervised methods:
Clustering (k-means, HAC)
Exploratory analysis of latent structures
Chapter V – Advanced Classification Methods
Generalised linear discriminant functions
Support Vector Machines (SVMs): margins, kernels
Neural networks: multilayer perceptron, backpropagation
Comparison between classical methods and advanced models
Assessment
Final examination (in person): 60%
Continuous assessment (CC): 40% — written tests, personal work
Bibliography
NAKACHE J.P., CONFAIS J. (2000).
Méthodes de classification
. CISIA-CERESTA.
